name: Garlic

book_claims.claim: Provides phytochemicals with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties as part of Mediterranean diet pattern
book_claims.mechanism: Allicin and organosulfur compounds modulate inflammatory pathways

book_claims.claim: Regular garlic-powder intake has protective effect against hypertension and age-dependent arterial stiffening
book_claims.mechanism: When crushed, alliin is exposed to alliinase producing allicin, which lowers blood pressure and cholesterol. Diallyl sulphide inhibits bowel cancer incidence by 74% in mice.

book_claims.claim: Associated with reduced risk of gastric, colon, prostate and endometrial cancers
book_claims.mechanism: Multiple bioactive organosulphur compounds including allicin and diallyl sulphide

supplementary_research.finding: Aged garlic extract consistently reduces blood pressure by 7-16 mmHg systolic in hypertensive individuals. Also shows reliable reductions in total and LDL cholesterol. Evidence for cancer prevention is promising but mostly observational.

Meta-analysis of 11 RCTs found garlic supplementation significantly reduced systolic blood pressure by 4.6 mmHg and diastolic by 7.3 mmHg in hypertensive patients compared to placebo. Superior effect in hypertensive vs normotensive subjects.

Updated meta-analysis confirmed garlic reduces blood pressure in hypertensive patients (mean reduction 8.4 mmHg systolic, 7.3 mmHg diastolic). Aged garlic extract was the most consistently effective form.

Meta-analysis of case-control studies found high allium vegetable consumption (garlic, onion) associated with reduced risk of gastric cancer (OR 0.54, 95% CI 0.43-0.65), supporting the book's cancer risk reduction claims.

Aged garlic extract reduced coronary artery calcification progression and improved endothelial function, suggesting vascular anti-aging effects consistent with the book's claims about arterial stiffening prevention.

culinary_pairings.source: Foundation of aglio e olio and most Mediterranean sauces
Classic soffritto base; synergistic anti-cancer compounds (S-allylcysteine + lycopene)
Foundational aromatic duo in Chinese, Thai, and Indian cooking
Acid brightens garlic's pungency in dressings and marinades
Persillade (garlic + parsley) is a classic French finishing condiment
Heat and pungency amplify each other across many cuisines
Garlic butter (beurre a l'ail) for bread, shellfish, and vegetables
Classic herb pairing for roasted meats and vegetables
Umami base for stir-fries, marinades, and dipping sauces
Foundational spice combination in curries, hummus, and legume dishes

seasonality: Fresh garlic harvested mid-summer (July-August in Northern Hemisphere). Green/spring garlic available April-June. Cured garlic stores well and is available year-round. Black garlic is fermented and shelf-stable.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Up to 4.5 mg per crushed clove (~3g); unstable, degrades within hours
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Formed only when alliin contacts alliinase during crushing/chopping. Destroyed by heat within minutes. Let crushed garlic sit 10 minutes before cooking to maximize allicin formation and conversion to stable thiosulfinates.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 0.5-1.5 mg in raw garlic; 5-10 mg in aged garlic extract per gram
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Highly bioavailable (over 90% absorption). Water-soluble and stable. Primary active compound in aged garlic extract. Synergistic with lycopene from tomatoes against gastric cancer.
Oil-soluble organosulfur compound. More heat-stable than allicin. Active in cancer prevention pathways (histone deacetylase inhibition).
Garlic is one of the richest vegetable sources of selenium. Bioavailability is high as selenomethionine.
Important cofactor for antioxidant enzyme superoxide dismutase (MnSOD).

synergies.description: Garlic (S-allylcysteine) + tomatoes (lycopene) inhibit stomach cancer at lower doses than either alone
